creator: jorhett@bitchandwine.net
text:
On Jan 8, 2008, at 3:50 AM, Eygene Ryabinkin wrote:
> May I first note that the port maintainer said 'USE_BDB?=   44',
> so this can be overriden by the administrator.  I decided to use
> 4.3 at the time of the PR submission, because the system used BDB
> 4.3 everywhere and cfengine works with that version just fine.
> There is no strict technical reasons why not to use 4.4, but I found
> no reasons not to use the older BDB packages.

You say works, then say patches are necessary?  Not trying to give  
you a hard time, but if patches are necessary then it doesn't work.

> The CFEngine manual at http://www.cfengine.org/docs/cfengine- 
> Reference.html

is very often out of date ;-)  Best to submit patches for that.

> So BDB >= 3.2 will go, if the manual is not very outdated.  Moreover,
> Mark Burgess accepted those patches, so I believe that they are  
> correct.

Ah, that's the crucial part.  If true, then it's patched at the source.

> If you will read my follow-up from 3rd of August 2007, the port
> does not honour the Berkeley DB version I requested via WITH_BDB_VER
> and it linked the port against 4.0.

I didn't object to this part of the patch ;-)  I submit a fair amount  
of those same fixes.

> Yes, the changes were accepted to the mainstream CFEngine.  But at
> the time of submission there were no releases with that patches,
> so the best I can do is to submit the patches for the FreeBSD port
> to make it work 'just now' for other people, not just for me.

Right now we have a problem in that far too many things that should  
be fixed in the mainline code are being patched locally.  I'd rather  
get all of those fixes into the main distribution (I can help) and  
minimize our local patches if possible.

--
Jo Rhett
Bitch and Wine.net - nothing like cool glass of wine and a warm dog
